mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% and remains there for at least one month, the zero-lower-bound constraint becomes binding and forward guidance effects dominate. Equity valuations expand as real discount rates compress, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.13% indicates ultra-loose monetary stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window in ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk. Equity investors reprice downward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the signal propagates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in real retail sales (RSAFS adjusted for CPIAUCSL inflation) over a 3-month window signals robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations. This demand signal drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days as consensus upgrades propagate through the market.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with stable inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC between real retail sales growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX underperforms in more than 50% of 3-month periods with positive real sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.3%, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. Equity investors reduce growth expectations and increase risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while the federal funds rate remains above 4%, the market interprets this as evidence of policy-induced labor market softening without offsetting monetary accommodation. This triggers negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term (UNRATE_change × FEDFUNDS_level) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate by more than 5% month-over-month, signaling strong forward demand expectations and construction sector confidence. This demand signal propagates to equity valuations through multiplier effects and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with accommodative monetary conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances following HOUST accelerations > 5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, the market perceives a policy credibility gap. Real rates turn sharply negative, triggering equity repricing as inflation expectations become unanchored and discount rates become unstable, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: inflation surprise with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL_momentum × FEDFUNDS_level) has Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized over a quarter while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously decline by more than 3% month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand weakness across both consumption and investment channels. This dual-channel deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term (GDPC1_growth × HOUST_momentum) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
